question: A town's little league program has 108 baseballs, 54 bats, and 72 cases of sunflower seeds left over from last year. The items will be distributed to each team in the league. If each team receives an equal number of each item and there are no items remaining, what is the greatest possible number of teams in the little league program?
A league has 108 baseballs, 54 bats, and 72 cases to distribute equally to teams with none left over. What is the greatest number of teams?

solution.strategy: Greatest number of teams = GCF(a, b, c).
